# MLX Porter

> MLX Porter is a public, evidence-gated field manual and Agent Skill for inspecting, porting, validating, and optimizing PyTorch and Hugging Face models for Apple MLX on Apple Silicon.

Four real checkpoints port end to end inside the installable skill — Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct (dense decoder), BGE-base (BERT encoder), t5-small (encoder-decoder), and HuBERT-base (ASR) — and six architecture families have executable scaffolds. Beyond the payload, a one-off architecture stress test parity-proves all seven major architecture classes (adding ModernBERT, a Qwen3.5-text linear-attention hybrid, SmolVLM, and Granite-MoE) and records four measured quantization optimize receipts; those proof artifacts live on GitHub, not on this site. The other routes provide tested intake, planning, generic validation, and runbooks; they are not claims of completed real-model ports, and every performance claim stays withheld until a local receipt passes the promotion gate.

## Start here

- [Project overview](https://mlx-porter.pages.dev/): Capabilities, workflow, architecture routes, and evidence policy.
- [Public field manual](https://mlx-porter.pages.dev/docs/): MLX fundamentals, porting workflow, architecture atlas, parity gates, optimization guidance, and validation commands.

## Core technical documentation

- [Agent Skill contract](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Amal-David/mlx-porting-skill/main/mlx-model-porting/SKILL.md): Compact trigger map and operating contract for MLX model-porting work.
- [Porting core](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Amal-David/mlx-porting-skill/main/mlx-model-porting/references/porting-core.md): Source inspection, graph translation, explicit weight conversion, and staged parity method.
- [Model support map](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Amal-David/mlx-porting-skill/main/mlx-model-porting/references/model-support-map.md): Controlled architecture families, support boundaries, and routing guidance.
- [Parity and testing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Amal-David/mlx-porting-skill/main/mlx-model-porting/references/parity-and-testing.md): Correctness ladder and failure-localization guidance.
- [Benchmarking](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Amal-David/mlx-porting-skill/main/mlx-model-porting/references/benchmarking.md): Reproducible benchmark metadata, quality gates, and rollback conditions.

## Evidence and limitations

- [Validation contract](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Amal-David/mlx-porting-skill/main/VALIDATION.md): What the checked-in validation proves and what remains outside its scope.
- [Evidence index](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Amal-David/mlx-porting-skill/main/EVIDENCE_INDEX.md): Audited primary sources and recorded review depth.
- [Research report](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Amal-David/mlx-porting-skill/main/RESEARCH_REPORT.md): Architecture, research method, current capability boundary, and extension paths.

## Optional

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/Amal-David/mlx-porting-skill): Source, scripts, tests, examples, and release history.
- [Contribution standard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Amal-David/mlx-porting-skill/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): Provenance, review, testing, and optimization requirements.
